solutions.solution: a; Obviously, the author assumes that the board will be functioning after the date of the meeting one year later, but there is no hint that the Board will be dissolved after that year later. So, I is implicit. But it is, otherwise why would they assume that it is an easy subject, because schools are a place of elementary education, but colleges are taught at colleges. But he might not say anything about jobs. So, I is implicit, II is not.
If one says that corruption should be taught at schools, he must have assumed that it is an easy subject, because schools are a place of elementary education.

If major drugs are being envisaged, it must have been assumed that our present education system is inconsistent with what the nation needs. So, I is implicit. Again, if it is implicit, II also is, because II says the same as I.

If before he join a particular institute, A must have assumed that the particular institute was a good institute. So, I is implicit. While advising B, immediately he advises him to listen to A's advice so, II is implicit.

The mother only asks her son to drop the letter. I would have been implicit if she had asked her son to drop the letter and write the address also. But if is implicit. Had the mother not assumed that her son would comply, she wouldn't have asked him to do the job.
People love reading adventure stories but this does not mean that they have nothing else to read. Nor does it mean that they do not like to read anything else. So, both I and II are not implicit.
When someone says 'read this notice' he must have assumed that the other person can read. This would be possible only if the person is literate and not blind. So, both I and II are implicit.

If extra income were taken from the rich and given to the poor, what would happen? Obviously, it would result in equality of income. But the statement says that equality of income is necessary for maximising the utility. So, I is implicit. But II is not. It is a different kind of thing. 'Equal pay for equal work' allows, in a way, for 'unequal pay if unequal work' - far the statement highlights equality of income (whether equal work or unequal).
The statement does not mention anything about youngsters. So, I is not implicit. It is just the opposite of what the statement says.

If a photographer claim to make a person beautiful, even if he is not, he means that he would create a beautiful photograph of that person. What else can a photographer do? Had he been a plastic surgeon, the statement might have meant that he would really face-lift the person, but it only means that the photographer meant he is that he would create a beautiful photograph of a person even if he is not beautiful. Hence, I is implicit. If them beautiful?

The statement only highlights the quality of one shop; it does not talk about others. So, I is not implicit. But II is, otherwise why would they have highlighted 'you get your money's worth' in their advertisement? What is advertised and highlighted must be desirable.
Is implied. Otherwise, why would India import for the continuous eating factories? II is obvious.

The advertisement highlights the fact that the honey is natural. This means that non-natural honey is also possible. So, I is implicit. But the advertisement never touches the cost aspect of honey. So, II is not implicit.

Detergents are being recommended to clean clothes. This implies that they must be having the ability to clean. Now, ability to clean means the ability to dislodge grease and dirt, it does not mean the ability to form more lather. So, I is not implicit, but II is.

When the manager stresses the need to appoint more clerks, it must be because more clerks are needed. This means that the present staff is not sufficient. But this does not mean that the present staff is inefficient. So, it is not implicit. Obviously, more clerks are available, otherwise the question of appointing more clerks would not have arisen.

The manager asks his subordinate to go for the meeting if he is not well. This implies that if he is well, the manager would like to attend the meeting himself. So, II is implicit. Also, since, according to the manager, his subordinate can attend that meeting, it is obvious that the meeting can be attended by persons having a lower rank than managers. So I is implicit.

The advice is given on the behaviour that should be followed to pass the exam. This shows that passing exams is necessary. So, assumption I is implicit. Passing the examination is a form of success. And, according to the statement, it may be achieved by hard work, so assumption II is implicit.
It is not necessary that the son does not inform about his late-coming.

The parents may be afraid that, unless specified, he may not come. Aluminium is described as a versatile metal but it is not necessarily the only versatile metal. But II is implicit. If no company would be using aluminium for packaging, there would have been no advertisement.

If the President has assured people something, we may assume that 'something' was making people apprehensive. But we cannot add anything of our own. The President has assured people of elections in five years. This means that people were apprehensive about the elections being held every five years. We cannot conclude that people were apprehensive that elections would not be held after all.
It is not hinted in the statement. It is. The author says that apples cannot be good because they are 'too cheap: this means that he thinks that cheap things are usually not good.
